notes: This configuration resolves zoning administered by Maricopa County for land outside the corporate limits of a municipality by intersecting runtime coordinates with Layer 11 of the County's public PlanNet map service.

The official ArcGIS item describes the dataset as zoning for unincorporated Maricopa County, states that it reflects changes approved by the Maricopa County Board of Supervisors, and reports a two-week refresh cycle.

The query is restricted to JURIS = 'COUNTY'. A distinct-value inspection of the current layer returned COUNTY as its only jurisdiction value, providing an additional safeguard against using County zoning for incorporated parcels.

The official ArcGIS item still references Layer 21 from an earlier PlanNet service layout. The live service catalog currently exposes Zoning as Layer 11; the configured layer number, fields, and fixture were verified against the current service.

ZONE is the County-maintained base-zoning code. APN and EDIT_DATE are retained when populated, but they may be null on generalized zoning polygons; Skyesoft's parcel resolver remains the authoritative workflow for parcel identification.

The current source publishes RU-43, RU-70, and RU-190 while the County ordinance styles the corresponding districts as Rural-43, Rural-70, and Rural-190. Skyesoft preserves the source codes and normalizes only their descriptions.

Descriptions are normalized from the current Maricopa County Zoning Ordinance. Legacy or source-specific values AD-1, AD-2, AD-3, IND-2p, and IND-3p are intentionally left without inferred descriptions pending confirmation of their current legal labels.
The configured commercial fixture at 3655 W Anthem Way in unincorporated Anthem resolves to C-2, Intermediate Commercial.

The directory and jurisdiction label use maricopacounty and Maricopa County so resolveZoning() can consume the parcel jurisdiction directly; the source itself is limited to unincorporated County territory.

A resolved base-zoning result supports permit research but does not replace review of overlays, planned-area developments, plans of development, zoning cases, special-use permits, stipulations, military-airport compatibility constraints, floodplain requirements, or confirmation by the Maricopa County Planning and Development Department.
